_meta.description: Paired-naming dictionary for religious sites — SEO-04, AUD-017..AUD-020 data layer. Authored 2026-05-11 per plan 04 Task 2.
_meta.convention: Names follow BBC/AP paired-on-first-reference style (PITFALLS §3.1). Hebrew + English mandatory; Arabic where relevant (contested sites, mosques, Christian sites with Arabic readership). Wikidata IDs cross-reference the schema.org sameAs field.

western-wall.notes: SEO-04: NEVER use 'Wailing Wall' (archaic, mildly pejorative). 'Kotel' acceptable in context. Commercial photography requires Western Wall Heritage Foundation permit (see PITFALLS §5.4).

temple-mount.notes: SEO-04 paired-naming on first reference: 'Temple Mount / Haram al-Sharif' (EN), 'הר הבית / אל-חרם א-שריף' (HE). AUD-019: regex catches Temple Mount without nearby Haram al-Sharif. Religion field is null (contested → neutral framing per PITFALLS §3.1).

holy-sepulchre.denomination: Christian (shared by Greek Orthodox, Roman Catholic, Armenian Apostolic, Coptic Orthodox, Syriac Orthodox, Ethiopian Tewahedo — Status Quo of 1852)
holy-sepulchre.notes: Commercial photography requires acknowledgment; restrictedSiteAcknowledgment required in photo-credits.json (IMG-05). Six denominations share access via the Status Quo.

dome-of-the-rock.notes: On the Temple Mount / Haram al-Sharif complex. restrictedSiteAcknowledgment required per IMG-05 (Wakf permission for non-Muslim entry — entry to Muslims only since 2003).

al-aqsa-mosque.notes: Third-holiest site in Islam. On Temple Mount / Haram al-Sharif complex; entry restricted to Muslims by Wakf.

garden-tomb.notes: 19th-century Protestant alternative crucifixion/burial site; not historically authenticated but a major Protestant pilgrim destination.

tower-of-david.notes: Museum + historic citadel — Place rather than ReligiousBuilding (no current religious function). administrativeStatus=east-jerusalem (within Old City walls).

yad-vashem.notes: Holocaust memorial and research institution; not a religious building but treated as memorial-religious heritage. Free admission; photography restrictions inside exhibition halls.

mount-of-olives.notes: Multi-faith significance (Judaism: ancient cemetery; Christianity: Ascension); religion=null to remain neutral. Treat as Place rather than ReligiousBuilding.

cenacle.notes: Traditional site of the Last Supper, in Mount Zion.

city-of-david.notes: Archaeological site overlapping with the Palestinian neighborhood of Silwan; controversy noted in editorial guidelines but not partisan-coded here.

basilica-of-annunciation.notes: Catholic site in Nazareth (Lower Galilee); major Christian pilgrim destination.

church-of-multiplication.notes: On the Sea of Galilee shore; traditional site of the feeding-of-the-5000 miracle.

mount-of-beatitudes.notes: Traditional site of the Sermon on the Mount. Franciscan-administered.

capernaum.notes: Fishing-town archaeological site on Sea of Galilee; 'town of Jesus' per Synoptic gospels. Includes ancient synagogue ruins.

church-of-nativity.denomination: Christian (shared by Greek Orthodox, Armenian Apostolic, Roman Catholic)
church-of-nativity.notes: AUD-020 enforcement entry: Bethlehem is in PA Area A. Site is administered under PA security control. Editorial guidance: refer to it as 'Bethlehem (West Bank)' on first reference. UNESCO World Heritage.

bahai-gardens.notes: IMG-05 image rights RESTRICTED — Baháʼí World Centre requires advance written permission for any commercial photography (see Phase 2.3 / press@bahai.org gap in SUMMARY §6.3). UNESCO World Heritage.

stella-maris-monastery.notes: Hilltop Carmelite monastery in Haifa; cave traditionally associated with Elijah.

el-jazzar-mosque.notes: 18th-century Ottoman mosque in Akko Old City; UNESCO World Heritage site.

tomb-of-maimonides.notes: Jewish pilgrimage site in Tiberias; resting place of Moses ben Maimon (1138-1204).

mount-sodom.notes: Geological salt-rock formation by Dead Sea; place not religious building. religion=null.

masada.notes: UNESCO World Heritage cliff-top fortress; historical-religious significance but treat as Place (use buildPlace, not buildReligiousBuilding).

qumran.notes: Site of Dead Sea Scrolls discovery; in Area C of West Bank under Israeli administration. AUD-020: noted location for editorial transparency.

mount-bental.notes: Extinct volcanic cone in Golan Heights; UN-disputed status. Place (lookout/observation, not religious). administrativeStatus=golan-heights triggers AUD-020 transparency.

sea-of-galilee.notes: Freshwater lake (lowest freshwater lake on Earth); religious significance for Christianity (Jesus's ministry shore) but treated as Place. Israel's primary drinking-water reservoir.

yardenit.denomination: Christian (ecumenical — Greek Orthodox jurisdiction; widely used by Protestant + Catholic pilgrim groups)
yardenit.notes: Symbolic Jordan River baptismal site near the southern Sea of Galilee outflow. Operated by Kibbutz Kinneret; immersion robes for rent. Ecumenical use across Christian denominations. Galilee Plan 03-03 PlaceOfWorship emission canary.
